---
title: "Syllabus"
---

::: {.callout-important}
The **official syllabus** is the PDF below. The HTML summary at the bottom is for quick reference and search only.
:::

::: {.syllabus-actions}
[Download PDF](files/syllabus.pdf){.btn-download}
[Open in new tab](files/syllabus.pdf){.btn-secondary target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.pdf-viewer}
::: {.pdf-viewer-header}
Official syllabus — MGT 816 · Fall 2026
:::

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="files/syllabus.pdf#view=FitH"
  title="MGT 816 Fall 2026 Syllabus"
  loading="lazy">
  <p>Your browser cannot display PDFs inline.
    <a href="files/syllabus.pdf">Download the syllabus (PDF)</a> instead.</p>
</iframe>
```
:::

::: {.callout-note}
**On a phone or tablet?** If the preview does not load, use **Download PDF** or **Open in new tab** above — most mobile browsers handle PDFs better that way.
:::

---

## Quick reference (HTML summary)

### Course description

This course is about the CFO role at a private, entrepreneurial company. We cover accounting-based financial metrics, financial reporting, and managerial accounting, and we build the accounting system the way a company does: from the start-up phase through to exit.

**Prerequisites:** Financial Accounting and Corporate Finance.

**Textbook:** *Entrepreneurial Finance and Accounting for High-Tech Companies*, Frank Fabozzi, MIT Press, 2016 (first edition).

### Grading (400 points total)

| Component | Points |
|:----------|-------:|
| Final exam (closed book; one handwritten cheat sheet) | 200 |
| Two problem sets (groups of 2–4; both count) | 80 |
| Two cases (groups of up to 4) | 75 |
| Class participation (in-class + pre-class Canvas polls) | 45 |
| **Total** | **400** |

The course cannot be taken pass/fail.

### Deliverables at a glance

| Deliverable | Assigned | Due (1:00 pm) | Points |
|:------------|:---------|:--------------|-------:|
| Case 1 memo (FeedBurner) | Wed, Sep 2 | Wed, Sep 16 | 37.5 |
| Problem Set 1 (cap tables) | Wed, Sep 9 | Wed, Sep 23 | 40 |
| Case 2 memo (Solstice Robotics) | Mon, Sep 21 | Wed, Sep 30 | 37.5 |
| Problem Set 2 (AI-assisted forecast) | Mon, Sep 28 | Mon, Oct 12 | 40 |
| Final exam | — | Fri, Oct 16, 5:30–8:30 pm | 200 |

### Use of AI

AI tools are **required** on Problem Set 2 and welcome as study aids throughout the course. Two rules apply:

1. **Every number you submit is yours** — if you cannot reproduce and defend it, do not submit it. On PS2 you document this in a graded verification log.
2. **The final exam is closed book** and tests this material without AI.

### Laptop and tablet use

Tablets are permitted for notes and following lecture materials. Laptops may be used only in the back rows, with one exception: **Monday, September 28** (AI-assisted forecasting workshop) — bring a laptop with Excel; laptops are required throughout the room that day.

### Full schedule

See the [Schedule](schedule.qmd) page for session-by-session preparation and links.
